purpose: FLAGSHIP B3: EARLY ROUTING on the real-checkpoint offload pipeline. B2 measured Qwen3-235B's token-to-token expert stickiness at 0.43-0.45 and established the bandwidth law (full-k speculation at hit rate H costs (2-H)x bytes -> negative below H~0.6-0.7). B3 replaces the predictor: layer L+1's REAL router weights applied to L+1's PRE-ATTENTION hidden (available the moment L's MoE lands; same token, same residual stream). Registered questions: (1) the pre/post-attention top-8 agreement rate A of a production 235B router (the science, reported unbarred), (2) whether the copy now overlaps attention enough to beat the serialized baseline, (3) math untouched.
frozen_commit: 5f17cd4 (grouped-nf4-gemm main). No changes between stamp and results.

protocol.host: RunPod SECURE H100 class (>=150 GB RAM, >=600 GB volume); on-box link microbench as in B2.
protocol.runs: SMOKE first (--layers 4, real checkpoint, paired off/early, 8 tokens) to validate the loop without burning a full build; then full 94-layer: per prompt (3 fixed), prefetch-OFF (64 greedy tokens) then EARLY (128), same process, KV+state reset between. Corrections ride a priority CUDA stream (the owned B2 queueing flaw, fixed).
protocol.reduction: median s/token per mode; agreement A = fraction of expert-slots correctly predicted across all EARLY steps.

frozen_verdict_criteria.E1_identity: EARLY greedy tokens identical to OFF for the paired 64-token prefix, all 3 prompts.
frozen_verdict_criteria.E2_paired: median tok/s(EARLY) >= 1.05 x tok/s(OFF) on every prompt.
frozen_verdict_criteria.E3_waterfall: tok/s(EARLY) >= 0.80 x the on-box waterfall ceiling on every prompt.
frozen_verdict_criteria.REPORT: agreement rate A per prompt (pre-attention router vs true router, top-8); bytes overhead (2-A); absolute tok/s; verbatim texts + distinct-2.
frozen_verdict_criteria.no_tune_clause: Failures reported at full volume; claims narrowed; no re-runs until green.
